response: [{"id":"0021fbd1-3a7a-4a4c-bd31-306971f05fcb","name":"Kneeling Cable Triceps Extension","description":"Exercise: Kneeling Cable Triceps Extension","instructions":["Place a bench sideways in front of a high pulley machine.","Hold a straight bar attachment above your head with your hands about 6 inches apart with your palms facing down.","Face away from the machine and kneel.","Place your head and the back of your upper arms on the bench. Your elbows should be bent with the forearms pointing towards the high pulley. This will be your starting position.","While keeping your upper arms close to your head at all times with the elbows in, press the bar out in a semicircular motion until the elbows are locked and your arms are parallel to the floor. Contract the triceps hard and keep this position for a second. Exhale as you perform this movement.","Slowly return to the starting position as you breathe in.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"isolation","force":"push","equipment":"cable","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:56.514Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:56.514Z","images":[{"id":"387e1954-d2be-4c85-a9cc-74d52117c77a","url":"/exercises/Kneeling_Cable_Triceps_Extension/0.jpg","exerciseId":"0021fbd1-3a7a-4a4c-bd31-306971f05fcb"},{"id":"eb63dda1-88b8-4c35-a8b3-13357c41b9fb","url":"/exercises/Kneeling_Cable_Triceps_Extension/1.jpg","exerciseId":"0021fbd1-3a7a-4a4c-bd31-306971f05fcb"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"4fb062fc-7803-4be6-8e35-9d4521d48a69","name":"triceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"0090ba34-ba48-4e3b-a423-3f16ff4ffdc2","name":"Bent Over Barbell Row","description":"Exercise: Bent Over Barbell Row","instructions":["Holding a barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing down), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward, by bending at the waist, while keeping the back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep the head up. The barbell should hang directly in front of you as your arms hang perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.","Now, while keeping the torso stationary, breathe out and lift the barbell to you. Keep the elbows close to the body and only use the forearms to hold the weight. At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a brief pause.","Then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"compound","force":"pull","equipment":"barbell","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:43.222Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:43.222Z","images":[{"id":"0505cb17-84f7-4ce9-b234-a1bd4aa1c215","url":"/exercises/Bent_Over_Barbell_Row/0.jpg","exerciseId":"0090ba34-ba48-4e3b-a423-3f16ff4ffdc2"},{"id":"d699cc9e-c865-43be-bfd6-57ae6ceb7f57","url":"/exercises/Bent_Over_Barbell_Row/1.jpg","exerciseId":"0090ba34-ba48-4e3b-a423-3f16ff4ffdc2"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"c1010ae8-1955-40c0-b867-cb54f547e865","name":"middle back","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"b3518531-493b-4b4b-b683-ffca8a860f34","name":"shoulders","target":null},{"id":"4214762b-72e2-4ee4-ad61-3029d754c987","name":"biceps","target":null},{"id":"43f709ed-5aae-45dc-9ac8-46bb72114d4d","name":"lats","target":null}],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"01223d0f-422b-42b9-8b33-28d5a21afa68","name":"Speed Box Squat","description":"Exercise: Speed Box Squat","instructions":["Attach bands to the bar that are securely anchored near the ground. You may need to choke the bands to get adequate tension.","Use a box of an appropriate height for this exercise. Load the bar to a weight that still requires effort, but isn't so heavy that speed is compromised. Typically, that will be between 50-70% of your one rep max.","Position the bar on your upper back, shoulder blades retracted, back arched and everything tight head to toe. This will be the starting position.","Unrack the bar and position yourself in front of the box. Sit back with your hips until you are seated on the box, ensuring that you descend under control and don't crash onto the surface.","Pause briefly, and explode off of the box, extending through the hips and knees."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"compound","force":"push","equipment":"barbell","categoryId":"e4bc27dd-0901-4008-8ad2-3029a0980acc","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:09.911Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:09.911Z","images":[{"id":"f798788c-f5be-415e-8c16-e1f19c8c0c72","url":"/exercises/Speed_Box_Squat/0.jpg","exerciseId":"01223d0f-422b-42b9-8b33-28d5a21afa68"},{"id":"b030cd57-919e-4352-930f-3259963623be","url":"/exercises/Speed_Box_Squat/1.jpg","exerciseId":"01223d0f-422b-42b9-8b33-28d5a21afa68"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"b2143318-8c41-4777-bd36-c87ef34d8e2f","name":"quadriceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"0d24eb4b-c01e-4b2a-ab6b-366de8652174","name":"hamstrings","target":null},{"id":"7ca25108-3887-48ab-a850-3246065dc979","name":"calves","target":null},{"id":"590af2ce-c0a2-44b2-9c5d-b20da6651b37","name":"glutes","target":null}],"category":{"id":"e4bc27dd-0901-4008-8ad2-3029a0980acc","name":"powerlifting"}},{"id":"0262ecbf-c902-4779-a9f5-de1dffcd4cde","name":"Calf Press On The Leg Press Machine","description":"Exercise: Calf Press On The Leg Press Machine","instructions":["Using a leg press machine, sit down on the machine and place your legs on the platform directly in front of you at a medium (shoulder width) foot stance.","Lower the safety bars holding the weighted platform in place and press the platform all the way up until your legs are fully extended in front of you without locking your knees. (Note: In some leg press units you can leave the safety bars on for increased safety. If your leg press unit allows for this, then this is the preferred method of performing the exercise.) Your torso and the legs should make perfect 90-degree angle. Now carefully place your toes and balls of your feet on the lower portion of the platform with the heels extending off. Toes should be facing forward, outwards or inwards as described at the beginning of the chapter. This will be your starting position.","Press on the platform by raising your heels as you breathe out by extending your ankles as high as possible and flexing your calf. Ensure that the knee is kept stationary at all times. There should be no bending at any time. Hold the contracted position by a second before you start to go back down.","Go back slowly to the starting position as you breathe in by lowering your heels as you bend the ankles until calves are stretched.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"push","equipment":"machine","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:45.258Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:45.258Z","images":[{"id":"e10d1f40-7f70-4a57-a600-80e8b52c0f7f","url":"/exercises/Calf_Press_On_The_Leg_Press_Machine/0.jpg","exerciseId":"0262ecbf-c902-4779-a9f5-de1dffcd4cde"},{"id":"18660bec-c4a2-407b-9937-033517c1ca8b","url":"/exercises/Calf_Press_On_The_Leg_Press_Machine/1.jpg","exerciseId":"0262ecbf-c902-4779-a9f5-de1dffcd4cde"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"7ca25108-3887-48ab-a850-3246065dc979","name":"calves","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"028b4a9f-9757-4b8c-b14d-20aea086edb5","name":"Dumbbell Floor Press","description":"Exercise: Dumbbell Floor Press","instructions":["Lay on the floor holding dumbbells in your hands. Your knees can be bent. Begin with the weights fully extended above you.","Lower the weights until your upper arm comes in contact with the floor. You can tuck your elbows to emphasize triceps size and strength, or to focus on your chest angle your arms to the side.","Pause at the bottom, and then bring the weight together at the top by extending through the elbows."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"compound","force":"push","equipment":"dumbbell","categoryId":"e4bc27dd-0901-4008-8ad2-3029a0980acc","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:49.234Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:49.234Z","images":[{"id":"4645d0ff-5ca3-46f0-a5a0-7a2559fcec75","url":"/exercises/Dumbbell_Floor_Press/0.jpg","exerciseId":"028b4a9f-9757-4b8c-b14d-20aea086edb5"},{"id":"02d5299d-a594-4a06-8ba5-c1f3159015c7","url":"/exercises/Dumbbell_Floor_Press/1.jpg","exerciseId":"028b4a9f-9757-4b8c-b14d-20aea086edb5"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"4fb062fc-7803-4be6-8e35-9d4521d48a69","name":"triceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"b3518531-493b-4b4b-b683-ffca8a860f34","name":"shoulders","target":null},{"id":"89aaeec9-faba-474a-a1e7-aeab72f42db3","name":"chest","target":null}],"category":{"id":"e4bc27dd-0901-4008-8ad2-3029a0980acc","name":"powerlifting"}},{"id":"02cd9762-a9ca-4edf-9572-b73be51bdf12","name":"Landmine Linear Jammer","description":"Exercise: Landmine Linear Jammer","instructions":["Position a bar into landmine or, lacking one, securely anchor it in a corner. Load the bar to an appropriate weight and position the handle attachment on the bar.","Raise the bar from the floor, taking the handles to your shoulders. This will be your starting position.","In an athletic stance, squat by flexing your hips and setting your hips back, keeping your arms flexed.","Reverse the motion by powerfully extending through the hips, knees, and ankles, while also extending the elbows to straighten the arms. This movement should be done explosively, coming out of the squat to full extension as powerfully as possible.","Return to the starting position."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"compound","force":"push","equipment":"barbell","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:56.863Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:56.863Z","images":[{"id":"542430cc-0416-4d69-8f30-1b15be589825","url":"/exercises/Landmine_Linear_Jammer/0.jpg","exerciseId":"02cd9762-a9ca-4edf-9572-b73be51bdf12"},{"id":"6a3d0167-bc49-40bd-ba89-186d8ecbf671","url":"/exercises/Landmine_Linear_Jammer/1.jpg","exerciseId":"02cd9762-a9ca-4edf-9572-b73be51bdf12"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"b3518531-493b-4b4b-b683-ffca8a860f34","name":"shoulders","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"df2b893d-a4ab-4e19-8c65-2481c4d82b35","name":"abdominals","target":null},{"id":"0d24eb4b-c01e-4b2a-ab6b-366de8652174","name":"hamstrings","target":null},{"id":"7ca25108-3887-48ab-a850-3246065dc979","name":"calves","target":null},{"id":"b2143318-8c41-4777-bd36-c87ef34d8e2f","name":"quadriceps","target":null},{"id":"4fb062fc-7803-4be6-8e35-9d4521d48a69","name":"triceps","target":null},{"id":"89aaeec9-faba-474a-a1e7-aeab72f42db3","name":"chest","target":null}],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"02f4f45b-36c4-41eb-be4a-8e2c1ce5aa87","name":"Smith Machine Calf Raise","description":"Exercise: Smith Machine Calf Raise","instructions":["Place a block or weight plate below the bar on the Smith machine. Set the bar to a position that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step onto the plates with the balls of your feet and place the bar on the back of your shoulders.","Take the bar with both hands facing forward. Rotate the bar to unrack it. This will be your starting position.","Raise your heels as high as possible by pushing off of the balls of your feet, flexing your calf at the top of the contraction. Your knees should remain extended. Hold the contracted position for a second before you start to go back down.","Return slowly to the starting position as you breathe in while lowering your heels.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"push","equipment":"machine","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:08.821Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:08.821Z","images":[{"id":"d0ba9e56-d7d9-4536-abf0-6fcda194eb7a","url":"/exercises/Smith_Machine_Calf_Raise/0.jpg","exerciseId":"02f4f45b-36c4-41eb-be4a-8e2c1ce5aa87"},{"id":"cdf2d588-01f2-45bd-8c27-35e8cb93e2f9","url":"/exercises/Smith_Machine_Calf_Raise/1.jpg","exerciseId":"02f4f45b-36c4-41eb-be4a-8e2c1ce5aa87"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"7ca25108-3887-48ab-a850-3246065dc979","name":"calves","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"03502592-fc94-4568-89b7-c5b64bffa3c2","name":"Cable Preacher Curl","description":"Exercise: Cable Preacher Curl","instructions":["Place a preacher bench about 2 feet in front of a pulley machine.","Attach a straight bar to the low pulley.","Sit at the preacher bench with your elbow and upper arms firmly on top of the bench pad and have someone hand you the bar from the low pulley.","Grab the bar and fully extend your arms on top of the preacher bench pad. This will be your starting position.","Now start pilling the weight up towards your shoulders and squeeze the biceps hard at the top of the movement. Exhale as you perform this motion. Also, hold for a second at the top.","Now slowly lower the weight to the starting position.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"pull","equipment":"cable","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:44.933Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:44.933Z","images":[{"id":"ac81eb53-bf24-4b59-a30c-297427ddbe25","url":"/exercises/Cable_Preacher_Curl/0.jpg","exerciseId":"03502592-fc94-4568-89b7-c5b64bffa3c2"},{"id":"7ce24740-c96b-46d2-bd1e-53675ef01244","url":"/exercises/Cable_Preacher_Curl/1.jpg","exerciseId":"03502592-fc94-4568-89b7-c5b64bffa3c2"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"4214762b-72e2-4ee4-ad61-3029d754c987","name":"biceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"5eea4d17-18b8-4890-9e8b-2a4976104915","name":"forearms","target":null}],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"03971c5f-08bf-48ea-9fe8-6d6fddbba36e","name":"Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl","description":"Exercise: Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl","instructions":["Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in your right hand.","Place your feet flat on the floor, at a distance that is slightly wider than shoulder width apart.","Lean forward and place your right forearm on top of your upper right thigh with your palm down. Tip: Make sure that the back of the wrist lies on top of your knees. This will be your starting position.","Lower the dumbbell as far as possible as you keep a tight grip on the dumbbell. Inhale as you perform this movement.","Now curl the dumbbell as high as possible as you contract the forearms and as you exhale. Keep the contraction for a second before you lower again. Tip: The only movement should happen at the wrist.","Perform for the recommended amount of repetitions, switch arms and repeat the movement."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"isolation","force":"pull","equipment":"dumbbell","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:06.534Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:06.534Z","images":[{"id":"246b07e2-c7c1-49ae-ad4c-97561ca9cfe9","url":"/exercises/Seated_One-Arm_Dumbbell_Palms-Down_Wrist_Curl/0.jpg","exerciseId":"03971c5f-08bf-48ea-9fe8-6d6fddbba36e"},{"id":"9d16f29d-9a34-4aa0-8b9a-e912ec74eaf8","url":"/exercises/Seated_One-Arm_Dumbbell_Palms-Down_Wrist_Curl/1.jpg","exerciseId":"03971c5f-08bf-48ea-9fe8-6d6fddbba36e"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"5eea4d17-18b8-4890-9e8b-2a4976104915","name":"forearms","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"03e92e76-50dd-42fe-964a-85f0d846f7c6","name":"On-Your-Back Quad Stretch","description":"Exercise: On-Your-Back Quad Stretch","instructions":["Lie on a flat bench or step, and hang one leg and arm over the side.","Bend the knee and hold the top of the foot. As you do this, be careful not to arch your lower back.","Pull the belly button to the spine to stay in neutral. Press your foot down and into your hand. To add the hip stretch, lift the hip of the leg you're holding up toward the ceiling.","Switch sides."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"static","equipment":"other","categoryId":"a6a36e08-04e3-49c4-90ba-1a2270643855","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:59.717Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:59.717Z","images":[{"id":"ee6eb9a8-8bbe-44df-99e7-15d8d940e102","url":"/exercises/On-Your-Back_Quad_Stretch/0.jpg","exerciseId":"03e92e76-50dd-42fe-964a-85f0d846f7c6"},{"id":"9e8e1ae2-19fc-480c-8c5b-ff1faafe392d","url":"/exercises/On-Your-Back_Quad_Stretch/1.jpg","exerciseId":"03e92e76-50dd-42fe-964a-85f0d846f7c6"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"b2143318-8c41-4777-bd36-c87ef34d8e2f","name":"quadriceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"a6a36e08-04e3-49c4-90ba-1a2270643855","name":"stretching"}},{"id":"0412cc79-5ef2-4a50-9a43-14c61d8176e4","name":"Natural Glute Ham Raise","description":"Exercise: Natural Glute Ham Raise","instructions":["Using the leg pad of a lat pulldown machine or a preacher bench, position yourself so that your ankles are under the pads, knees on the seat, and you are facing away from the machine. You should be upright and maintaining good posture.","This will be your starting position. Lower yourself under control until your knees are almost completely straight.","Remaining in control, raise yourself back up to the starting position.","If you are unable to complete a rep, use a band, a partner, or push off of a box to aid in completing a repetition."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"compound","force":"pull","equipment":"body only","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:59.500Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:59.500Z","images":[{"id":"268510cc-0e28-495e-8a4d-ba80c7258e58","url":"/exercises/Natural_Glute_Ham_Raise/0.jpg","exerciseId":"0412cc79-5ef2-4a50-9a43-14c61d8176e4"},{"id":"4e0e0fe8-7ac0-438e-8120-bc7bfb910fba","url":"/exercises/Natural_Glute_Ham_Raise/1.jpg","exerciseId":"0412cc79-5ef2-4a50-9a43-14c61d8176e4"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"0d24eb4b-c01e-4b2a-ab6b-366de8652174","name":"hamstrings","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[{"id":"7ca25108-3887-48ab-a850-3246065dc979","name":"calves","target":null},{"id":"590af2ce-c0a2-44b2-9c5d-b20da6651b37","name":"glutes","target":null},{"id":"706c61ba-4aa5-46f8-b888-687eb5611c11","name":"lower back","target":null}],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"043adffb-bb2b-4c1c-868f-64b0d653e564","name":"Backward Medicine Ball Throw","description":"Exercise: Backward Medicine Ball Throw","instructions":["This exercise is best done with a partner. If you lack a partner, the ball can be thrown and retrieved or thrown against a wall.","Begin standing a few meters in front of your partner, both facing the same direction. Begin holding the ball between your legs.","Squat down and then forcefully reverse direction, coming to full extension and you toss the ball over your head to your partner.","Your partner can then roll the ball back to you. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"compound","force":"push","equipment":"medicine ball","categoryId":"3f28d8f0-785b-4617-a773-8c3f60725675","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:41.273Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:41.273Z","images":[{"id":"7db24b28-6287-409a-a443-44dbdb358a1c","url":"/exercises/Backward_Medicine_Ball_Throw/0.jpg","exerciseId":"043adffb-bb2b-4c1c-868f-64b0d653e564"},{"id":"4613b445-0fdd-4a23-80b2-18d6bdc2a636","url":"/exercises/Backward_Medicine_Ball_Throw/1.jpg","exerciseId":"043adffb-bb2b-4c1c-868f-64b0d653e564"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"b3518531-493b-4b4b-b683-ffca8a860f34","name":"shoulders","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"3f28d8f0-785b-4617-a773-8c3f60725675","name":"plyometrics"}},{"id":"04b04516-8131-4c3b-bfc5-caad6a3fd003","name":"High Cable Curls","description":"Exercise: High Cable Curls","instructions":["Stand between a couple of high pulleys and grab a handle in each arm. Position your upper arms in a way that they are parallel to the floor with the palms of your hands facing you. This will be your starting position.","Curl the handles towards you until they are next to your ears. Make sure that as you do so you flex your biceps and exhale. The upper arms should remain stationary and only the forearms should move. Hold for a second in the contracted position as you squeeze the biceps.","Slowly bring back the arms to the starting position.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"intermediate","mechanic":"compound","force":"pull","equipment":"cable","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:53.235Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:53.235Z","images":[{"id":"c6993315-5c81-408a-8a5d-8f4c09a6240d","url":"/exercises/High_Cable_Curls/0.jpg","exerciseId":"04b04516-8131-4c3b-bfc5-caad6a3fd003"},{"id":"c1f08ae4-2447-4c7a-9be5-bdc557b8d24b","url":"/exercises/High_Cable_Curls/1.jpg","exerciseId":"04b04516-8131-4c3b-bfc5-caad6a3fd003"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"4214762b-72e2-4ee4-ad61-3029d754c987","name":"biceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"04f0d680-3b0f-4932-bff5-71a0cd6dba0c","name":"Knee Across The Body","description":"Exercise: Knee Across The Body","instructions":["Lie down on the floor with your right leg straight. Bend your left leg and lower it across your body, holding the knee down toward the floor with your right hand. (The knee doesn't need to touch the floor if you're tight.)","Place your left arm comfortably beside you and turn your head to the left. Imagine you have a weight tied to your tailbone. let your tailbone fall back toward the floor as your chest reaches in the opposite direction to stretch your lower back. Make sure that you keep the head up. The upper arms should be close to the torso and parallel to the floor while the forearms are pointing towards the floor as the hands hold the weights. Tip: There should be a 90-degree angle between the forearms and the upper arm. This is your starting position.","Keeping the upper arms stationary, use the triceps to lift the weights as you exhale until the forearms are parallel to the floor and the whole arms are extended. Like many other arm exercises, only the forearm moves.","After a second contraction at the top, slowly lower the dumbbells back to their starting position as you inhale.","Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"push","equipment":"dumbbell","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:10.939Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:19:10.939Z","images":[{"id":"336217f2-f43d-4c4c-aab8-30a19ba49223","url":"/exercises/Standing_Bent-Over_Two-Arm_Dumbbell_Triceps_Extension/0.jpg","exerciseId":"05975b7e-0ba6-4a16-a96e-1aa7b57bd7f5"},{"id":"c50dad1b-994f-489c-aedb-3f828e186502","url":"/exercises/Standing_Bent-Over_Two-Arm_Dumbbell_Triceps_Extension/1.jpg","exerciseId":"05975b7e-0ba6-4a16-a96e-1aa7b57bd7f5"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"4fb062fc-7803-4be6-8e35-9d4521d48a69","name":"triceps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"05a14a60-fa07-42ea-8c1d-8d955e787801","name":"Barbell Shrug","description":"Exercise: Barbell Shrug","instructions":["Stand up straight with your feet at shoulder width as you hold a barbell with both hands in front of you using a pronated grip (palms facing the thighs). Tip: Your hands should be a little wider than shoulder width apart. You can use wrist wraps for this exercise for a better grip. This will be your starting position.","Raise your shoulders up as far as you can go as you breathe out and hold the contraction for a second. Tip: Refrain from trying to lift the barbell by using your biceps.","Slowly return to the starting position as you breathe in.","Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions."],"difficulty":"beginner","mechanic":"isolation","force":"pull","equipment":"barbell","categoryId":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","createdAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:42.330Z","updatedAt":"2026-02-13T02:18:42.330Z","images":[{"id":"ea8e1a8b-7177-493f-82e7-28cbd67927d9","url":"/exercises/Barbell_Shrug/0.jpg","exerciseId":"05a14a60-fa07-42ea-8c1d-8d955e787801"},{"id":"0c7517aa-4ecd-4249-b96b-4bb9519aa8fc","url":"/exercises/Barbell_Shrug/1.jpg","exerciseId":"05a14a60-fa07-42ea-8c1d-8d955e787801"}],"primaryMuscles":[{"id":"855e958c-cc27-456d-bd2f-8f04f0524057","name":"traps","target":null}],"secondaryMuscles":[],"category":{"id":"f22ccfc3-ee7a-4bbb-a1e9-f0bfd4b78533","name":"strength"}},{"id":"05aacc89-7cc9-4e44-8795-fbe15bf36242","name":"Tire Flip","description":"Exercise: Tire Flip","instructions":["Begin by gripping the bottom of the tire on the tread, and position your feet back a bit.
